---
title: "Installing R Packages"
description: "How to install the R packages needed for data science work, verify your installation using the DataScienceExercises package, and set up LaTeX for PDF output."
author: "Claudius Gräbner-Radkowitsch"
date: 2026-06-16
date-modified: 2026-06-16
image: ../../assets/images/placeholder.png

learning-objectives:
  - "Install a set of required R packages using an installation script"
  - "Verify that the DataScienceExercises package and learnr exercises are working"
  - "Install tinytex via Quarto to enable PDF rendering"

categories:
  - R
  - BA
  - tutorial

level:
  - BA

content-type: tutorial

citation:
  type: entry
  container-title: "Methods Hub"
  title: "Installing R Packages"
  editor:
    - name: "Claudius Gräbner-Radkowitsch"
  url: https://euf-methodology-hub.netlify.app/resources/r-programming/installing-r-packages
---

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(out.width='90%')
```

## Video lecture

```{=html}
<div class="embed-container">
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/videoseries?si=UBJo1AiCblQZFtZD&amp;list=PLZDawQMrG1RImURlbldAF773MDRqeARNu" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```

## Installing R packages

After installing [R and R-Studio](installing-r-and-rstudio.qmd), you
still need to install a number of so called [R packages](https://r-pkgs.org/).
We will learn more about what packages are and how to use them later.
Nevertheless, it is strongly
recommended to install all the packages you will need over the
following semester already now. This way you make sure that everything is working,
and you save yourself from trouble during the semester. Moreover, installing
these packages is necessary to do the exercises provided after each session.

To install packages, a stable internet connection is required. Then, proceed
as follows:

1. Download the installation script provided by your course and save it in a
directory of your choice.
2. Open the file in R-Studio. To this end,
right-click on the file and select `Open with`, and then choose R-Studio.
3. Adjust the first line of the script to the OS you are using
(e.g. `"Mac"` or `"Windows"`).
4. Select all the lines and click on the button `Run`.
5. Check if the package `here` was installed. You will get the respective
message in the console.

If you encounter any problems, please make a screenshot and describe the problem
in detail when asking for help.

6. Select the rest of the script and run it as you did with the first lines.
If you get a success message at the end, all packages were installed successfully.

Note: you may see the following message during the installation process:

```
Do you want to install from sources the packages which need compilation?
```

I recommend typing `No` and pressing enter. If the installation is not successful
you might run the installation commands again and try responding with `Yes`,
which sometimes fixes the problem.

## Verify the usability of exercises

If everything worked until here you should be able to access the
exercise sets provided after each session via the `DataScienceExercises` package.
To make sure everything works as expected, please run the following command
in the console and press enter:

```{r, eval=FALSE}
learnr::run_tutorial(
  name = "Basics",
  package = "DataScienceExercises",
  shiny_args=list("launch.browser"=TRUE))
```

If the exercise window opens, everything is working correctly.

After you closed the window and the process is still active, just click on the
`Stop` button in R-Studio, and then click on the `Console` tab on the left
to return to the console.

See the [blog post on DataScienceExercises](../../blog/2026-06-16-datascienceexercises/index.qmd)
for full installation instructions for the exercises package.

## Installing LaTeX

To create PDF-documents you have to install a
[LaTeX distribution](https://www.latex-project.org/).
It is recommended to install the distribution
[tinytex](https://yihui.org/tinytex/),
no matter whether you already have a different distribution or not.

The easiest route is to install tinytex via Quarto. If you have not yet
installed Quarto, follow the instructions on
[this webpage](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/).

Then follow the instructions from [this tutorial](https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/pdf-engine.html).
What you essentially need to do is to open your terminal and issue
the following command:

```
quarto install tinytex
```

To make sure everything runs smoothly you should create a small test `.qmd` file
and try to render it as a PDF by clicking the `Render` button in R-Studio.

If this works you are all set. If there are any errors showing up, please
search for solutions online or ask for help.

### A common problem when installing tinytex on a Mac

If after the attempted installation of `tinytex` you see an error message about
missing paths, execute the following command in your R console within
R-Studio:

```{r, eval=FALSE}
tinytex::install_tinytex(force = TRUE)
```

Then close R-Studio and restart your computer. If the problem persists, please
open your Mac Terminal and enter the following commands:

```
sudo chown -R `whoami`:admin /usr/local/bin

~/Library/TinyTeX/bin/x86_64-darwin/tlmgr path add
```

Then install `tinytex` again as described above, restart your computer, and
try to render a PDF again.

<script src="https://giscus.app/client.js"
        data-repo="graebnerc/research-methodology-hub"
        data-repo-id="R_kgDOS7J-4w"
        data-category="Comments"
        data-category-id="DIC_kwDOS7J-484C_N51"
        data-mapping="pathname"
        data-strict="0"
        data-reactions-enabled="1"
        data-emit-metadata="0"
        data-input-position="bottom"
        data-theme="preferred_color_scheme"
        data-lang="en"
        crossorigin="anonymous"
        async>
</script>
